mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate held above 5% for a sustained period keeps the equity discount rate elevated relative to trailing earnings yields, exerting continuous downward pressure on S&P 500 valuations and producing negative forward returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and 20d forward SPX returns stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or high-rate periods show statistically insignificant return difference vs low-rate periods (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the 3-month moving average of UNRATE rises more than 0.5 percentage points above its prior 12-month low (a Sahm-rule-like threshold), it signals an incipient labor-market downturn that historically precedes recessions, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter as earnings expectations reprice.
candidate_trigger: 3-month avg UNRATE rises > 0.5pp above trailing 12-month minimum
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.7% as of 2024-01, near cycle lows
failure_condition: In triggered episodes, 60-day forward SPX returns are positive or statistically indistinguishable from unconditional baseline (t-test p > 0.05) in more than 50% of historical occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: The interaction of a high nominal FEDFUNDS rate with decelerating CPIAUCSL inflation implies a rising real policy rate, which tightens financial conditions beyond what either series indicates alone and suppresses S&P 500 returns over the next month; conversely, when both fall together the effect reverses.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS=5.33 while CPIAUCSL YoY growth is decelerating as of late 2023
failure_condition: Real-rate proxy (FEDFUNDS minus CPIAUCSL YoY change) shows Rank IC < 0.03 with 20d forward returns over any 2-year rolling window, or effect sign flips inconsistently across subperiods.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle (FEDFUNDS elevated and rising over prior 6 months), a decline in HOUST housing starts signals interest-rate-sensitive demand destruction that leads broader economic slowdown, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter; in non-tightening regimes the same HOUST decline carries no reliable signal.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS elevated (>4.5%) and HOUST declines > 5% over 3 months
failure_condition: During periods meeting the trigger, 60-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 across tightening-cycle subsamples.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained positive month-over-month trend in INDPRO reflects expanding industrial output and corporate revenue momentum, which supports short-term S&P 500 price appreciation over the following week as earnings revisions trend upward.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO trailing 3-month trend and 5-day forward SPX returns remains below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in RSAFS retail sales exceeding 0.5% signals weakening consumer demand, which foreshadows downward revisions to corporate revenue guidance and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Triggered RSAFS declines are followed by positive or flat 20-day SPX forward returns in more than 55% of historical episodes, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Joint deceleration in PAYEMS growth alongside a rising UNRATE indicates simultaneous slack building in both employment quantity and unemployment measures, a combination more predictive of equity market stress than either series alone, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS=157232K and UNRATE=3.7% as of snapshot
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS-deceleration/UNRATE-rise signal produces Rank IC below 0.03 with 20-day forward returns over any rolling 2-year window, or fails to outperform either single-series baseline.

mechanism_narrative: In a regime where CPIAUCSL inflation is decelerating toward target while FEDFUNDS remains elevated, markets price in an approaching easing cycle; anticipatory positioning drives positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter as investors front-run expected rate cuts.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL YoY growth falls below 3.5% while FEDFUNDS remains above 5%
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL=308.85 and FEDFUNDS=5.33 as of snapshot, inflation decelerating from 2022 peak
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns are negative or statistically indistinguishable from baseline (p > 0.05) in more than half of historical episodes meeting the disinflation-with-high-rates trigger.
